name: Get Better Faster Action Step Guide
description: FLS coaching playbook aligned to Teacher Rubric — action steps, coaching prompts, practice exercises, and real-time feedback cues

coaching_cycles.name: Strong Start — Fundamentals
coaching_cycles.description: Leaders observe daily (5-10 min), use Real Time Coaching, weekly O3 meetings. Fundamentals Lock = 90% on task in 5 consecutive observations.

coaching_cycles.name: Essential Content
coaching_cycles.description: Biweekly observations minimum. Focus on lesson internalization and content alignment.

coaching_cycles.name: ActMo
coaching_cycles.description: Active Monitoring focus. Goal: all students demonstrate understanding, 85% receive feedback on track to learning goals.

coaching_cycles.name: Final PMAP
coaching_cycles.description: Full observation, final performance map conversation.

steps.action: Plan critical routines and procedures moment-by-moment
steps.when: Teacher does not have clear routines established
steps.coaching_prompt: What is each step the teacher takes in this routine? What is the teacher doing and what are the students doing?
steps.practice: Complete template for key routines (call to attention, entry/exit, transitions, materials). Rehearse every routine in classroom setting.
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.action: Plan the roll-out for introducing the routine
steps.when: Routine is new for the students
steps.coaching_prompt: What will be the most difficult parts of the routine for students to master? How will you model this effectively?
steps.practice: Script the I Do, We Do, You Do of roll-out speeches. Keep language positive, enthusiastic, including a challenge.
steps.rtc_cue: If model is ineffective: 'Mr. Smith, am I following your model effectively?'

steps.when: Students are following the routine but it is inefficient
steps.coaching_prompt: What has been the challenge in implementing this routine? Where does the breakdown begin?
steps.practice: Revise plan minute-by-minute. Role play with teacher rolling out revised routine.
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.action: Do It Again & Cut It Short
steps.when: Students aren't performing a routine correctly
steps.coaching_prompt: What are the keys to running a Do It Again effectively? Why is it important to have students repeat?
steps.practice: Role play revised routine with same errors students make. Practice until flawless.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: Make a circle with your finger to cue Do It Again.

steps.when: Teacher's body language lacks leadership presence
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the value in communicating leadership with our body language?
steps.practice: Deliver opening routines while squaring up and standing still.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: shift body upward and arch shoulders.

steps.when: Teacher's tone lacks leadership presence
steps.coaching_prompt: Imagine saying 'It's time to leave' to three audiences — friend, symphony, fire. What's the value of the middle one?
steps.practice: Deliver opening routines in three registers. Practice maintaining formal register throughout.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: square up/stand still gesture + point to mouth.

steps.when: Teacher speaks without getting students' attention
steps.coaching_prompt: Why is it important to get everyone's attention before you give directions?
steps.practice: Deliver call to order. Practice using it two times if students are not on task.
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.when: Teacher's directions are unclear and use too many words
steps.coaching_prompt: What happened when you asked students to ___? What caused the dip in behavior?
steps.practice: Plan and rehearse key directions in a lesson.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: sign MVP Directions. Whisper: 'When I say go, at a level zero...'

steps.when: Students don't feel like teacher is paying attention to behavior
steps.coaching_prompt: How do the students know in this moment that you are monitoring their behavior?
steps.practice: Have teacher use Pastor's Perch to Be Seen Looking after giving instructions.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: Make gesture of Be Seen Looking yourself.

steps.when: Teacher is not noticing earliest actions of non-compliance
steps.coaching_prompt: At what moment do the first students begin to go off track? Which students are most often off task?
steps.practice: Identify hot spots and moments in lesson plan to scan. Role play student behavior.
steps.rtc_cue: Hold hand out over a hot spot at the moment you want teacher to notice.

steps.when: Teacher is stationary, lack of movement enables off-task behavior
steps.coaching_prompt: Where did the off-task behavior start? Where were you standing at that time?
steps.practice: Develop a pathway for walking around classroom, stopping at corners.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: point to a corner where they should stand.

steps.when: Lesson doesn't conform to time stamps
steps.coaching_prompt: How much time did you want to spend on the I Do? What kept us from sticking to that?
steps.practice: Practice teaching with a timer. Role play what to do when timer goes off.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: point at watch. Hand signal for minutes remaining.

steps.when: Students don't have urgency in completing work
steps.coaching_prompt: What actions can you take to go faster? How could you challenge students to work with greater purpose?
steps.practice: Have teacher transition using a countdown to work the clock.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: signal 5-4-3-2-1 with fingers for countdown.

steps.when: Teacher's tone when addressing management is overly negative
steps.coaching_prompt: How did the teacher get students to correct misbehaviors without being negative?
steps.practice: Role play students not following directions. Practice warm/strict tone.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: index card with plus sign. Whisper: 'Warm strict'

steps.when: Students who are off task don't respond to clear directions
steps.coaching_prompt: What does this teacher do after they give clear directions? How does that affect students' attention?
steps.practice: Role play: model off-task behavior while teacher narrates positive of another student.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: 'Narrate X3'

steps.action: Least-Invasive Intervention (proximity, eye contact, non-verbal, quick whisper, self-interrupt)
steps.when: Teacher uses corrections that draw more attention than necessary
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the advantage of starting with the least invasive intervention?
steps.practice: Role play different off-task behaviors. Escalate through intervention types.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: point to off-task students. Whisper: 'Use ___ intervention.'

steps.when: Consequences are not being implemented
steps.coaching_prompt: You gave clear directions, narrated X3 and did non-verbal cues but students were still off task. What could you do?
steps.practice: Script how/when to implement consequence. Practice full BMC to calm consequences.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal or whisper to implement consequence.

steps.action: Explicitly teach words and actions of politeness
steps.when: Students are not using polite words with one another
steps.coaching_prompt: What words would you like students to use when interacting?
steps.practice: Create poster of 'Words We Use To Be Polite.' Write lesson plan for rollout.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: Point to the poster.

steps.action: Explicitly teach words and actions of apology
steps.when: Students are not apologizing to one another
steps.coaching_prompt: What words would you like students to use when they need to apologize?
steps.practice: Create poster of 'Words We Use To Apologize.' Write lesson plan for rollout.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: Point to the poster.

steps.action: Teach response to consequences from teacher
steps.when: Students have unproductive arguments with teacher over consequences
steps.coaching_prompt: When students have a conflict with you, how do you want them to respond?
steps.practice: Create lesson plan. Create catch phrase (right time, right tone, right place).
steps.rtc_cue: Model: Remind student in class when they don't adhere.

steps.when: Students have unproductive arguments with one another
steps.coaching_prompt: When students have a conflict, how do you want them to respond?
steps.practice: Create lesson plan for peer conflict resolution. Create catch phrase.
steps.rtc_cue: Model: Remind student when they don't adhere.

steps.action: Model the routine for each new structure
steps.when: Students are confused during a structure
steps.coaching_prompt: Watch me model the rollout. What did I do and say? What's different from your current application?
steps.practice: Script out how/when teacher will roll out. Practice the rollout.
steps.rtc_cue: Model: Rollout of a Kagan structure for the teacher.

steps.when: Teacher is not implementing class-building
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the goal of class-building? What is holding you back?
steps.practice: Script two specific class-builders for the week. Practice rollout and implementation.
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.action: Use gambits at end of structures
steps.when: Teacher implements Kagan but doesn't prompt kind words to partner
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the purpose of using a gambit at the end of a Kagan structure?
steps.practice: Create gambit poster. Practice a Kagan strategy with gambits multiple times.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: Point to gambit poster. Whisper: 'Use your gambits!'

steps.when: Teacher struggles to identify whether students have reached mastery
steps.coaching_prompt: What do you want students to write on the exit ticket? What exactly? How will you recognize it?
steps.practice: Write exemplar/CFS for exit ticket. Guide teacher to make it optimally rigorous.
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.when: Teacher does not address key points for most important problems
steps.coaching_prompt: What are the most important must-do parts for this lesson? What are the key points?
steps.practice: Plan for most important parts and isolate key points. Practice lesson components.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: 'How does this connect to our key point of ___?'

steps.action: Script exemplar answers for student work (Right is Right)
steps.when: Teacher struggles to evaluate student responses in-the-moment
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the completely right answer? What answers would be only partially correct?
steps.practice: Script exact answer for every key question. Role play not calling partial answers 'right.'
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.when: Teacher does not plan for specific pedagogical moves
steps.coaching_prompt: What are the specific ways you want students engaged? How are you implementing them?
steps.practice: Script exact engagement method for every key question. Role play.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: Prompt for using an engagement strategy.

steps.when: Teacher does not plan specific accommodations
steps.coaching_prompt: What are the specific accommodations and modifications? How are you implementing them?
steps.practice: Plan lesson incorporating acc/mods for specific students. Practice implementing.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: Prompt for using a specific acc/mod.

steps.when: Teacher struggles to check if students understand important aspects
steps.coaching_prompt: What key part of the lesson did students not understand? Why? What was the misconception?
steps.practice: Identify key moments. Script a CFU for each. Role play using CFU.
steps.rtc_cue: N/A

steps.action: ActMo 1 — Create monitoring pathway for On Task Start
steps.when: Teacher isn't paying attention to whether students are starting work
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the purpose of monitoring? Which 2-3 students do you approach first? What's the ideal order?
steps.practice: Plan monitoring pathway: fast writers first, key students to spend more time with.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal or whisper: Cue teacher to monitor lap for On Task Start.

steps.action: ActMo 2 — Monitor quality of student work
steps.when: Teacher does not see patterns in student answers or adjust teaching
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the purpose of active monitoring during work time? How can it inform the rest of your lesson?
steps.practice: Develop note-taking template. Practice a lap with sample work. Note patterns.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: Share patterns you're seeing in student responses.

steps.action: ActMo 3 — Mark up student work and give feedback
steps.when: Few students are getting explicit feedback during independent practice
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the student experiencing when you're monitoring? How many know if they're on track?
steps.practice: Create feedback code (simple cues). Practice writing codes on student work.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: Prompt lap for feedback. Model.

steps.when: Few students are using feedback to adjust work
steps.coaching_prompt: Once you've written feedback, how do you know they've made an adjustment?
steps.practice: Create response code. Practice a lap checking student response to feedback.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: Prompt lap for response to feedback.

steps.when: Teacher is not combining trend-spotting, marking up work, and ensuring changes
steps.coaching_prompt: How could you look for overall trends while also marking up work AND ensuring students addressed feedback?
steps.practice: Practice full monitoring cycle. Address misconceptions when 50%+ miss key criteria.
steps.rtc_cue: Prompt which type of lap is missing.

steps.when: Teacher moves ahead without clear vision of which students comprehend
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the purpose of polling the room? When would be the best moments to poll?
steps.practice: Script key moments to poll the room into lesson plan.
steps.rtc_cue: Intervene: Poll the room.

steps.when: Teacher doesn't call on students who most need practice
steps.coaching_prompt: What was your purpose in calling on that student? Which students are ideal for each question?
steps.practice: Select best student for each question. Identify higher student backup if they struggle.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: 'Call on a [high/medium/low] student to check understanding.'

steps.when: Students aren't given a chance to correct their own errors
steps.coaching_prompt: What do you want students to say? Where do you anticipate they'll struggle?
steps.practice: Role play questioning sequence. Provide answers to practice rolling back.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: rolling/cranking motion (like 'movie' in charades).

steps.when: After incorrect response, teacher moves on or repeats original question
steps.coaching_prompt: What is the student experiencing when you respond to an error? What are the key components they must know?
steps.practice: Design scaffolded questions that break down upcoming lesson questions.
steps.rtc_cue: Signal when best move is to break it down. Model if needed.

steps.when: After incorrect answer, teacher doesn't return to original student
steps.coaching_prompt: When the student gave the incorrect answer, what was the missed opportunity?
steps.practice: Role play questioning sequence, coming back to first student.
steps.rtc_cue: Whisper: 'Come back to x student to make sure they understand.'

steps.when: Students lack opportunities to write before discussing
steps.coaching_prompt: What are the limitations of class discussion for assessing every student? How would writing first change the discussion?
steps.practice: Identify when to give Everybody Writes. Build Do Nows or exit tickets.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: write in the air. Intervene: 'Everybody write: [key question].'

steps.when: Teacher tends to call on same few students
steps.coaching_prompt: Let's look at footage — which students are you calling on? Which aren't?
steps.practice: Plan which students to call on and when, ensuring all levels get called.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: point to ideal student to call on.

steps.action: Rule of Thirds (cold call, taking hands, choral response)
steps.when: Teacher over-relies on one technique
steps.coaching_prompt: When is the best moment to use cold call vs. taking hands vs. choral response?
steps.practice: Role play whole group discussion using each strategy in succession.
steps.rtc_cue: Non-verbal: cue for cold call, choral response, all hands.
